behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure occurs at the handoff between evaluate-stage command logic and artifact storage slot selection (`grounding` vs `plan`). Integration-brake: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, so not System; correctness is slot routing, not model output quality, so not Agentic; no multi-agentic sequence required, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on artifact key destination (`grounding` artifact, not `plan` artifact), which can be validated with in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a real artifact repository implementation (in-memory/local test DB) and assert it writes only `grounding` for grounding output and leaves `plan` unchanged.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: this is a read/write contract across modules (implement flow + artifact store), and failures show up at interface handoff (read `grounding`, write `plan`). Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior is stated, so not System; behavior checks data plumbing, not LLM output quality, so not Agentic; no required multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: behavior text is about artifact availability and destination (`reads grounding when available` and `writes ... to plan`), which does not require higher-level infra.
behaviors.test_description: Pre-seed `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage flow, assert grounding is consumed (e.g., appears in internal context/inputs) and resulting execution artifact is written to `plan` without altering `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure depends on interaction between issue-content diagnosis detection and artifact-write side effects; bug manifests when modules hand off diagnosis state to persistence logic. Integration-brake: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess dependency, so not System; no AI output-quality assertion, so not Agentic; no multi-agentic sequence requirement, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: behavior text explicitly anchors on side-effect suppression based on existing issue content, which can be validated without higher-level infra.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage, assert no write/update occurs to `plan` artifact while other expected deep-dive outputs remain correct.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: this is a cross-step data propagation failure at module boundaries in the orchestration chain (stage outputs to later stage inputs). Integration-brake: although text says "full multi-step agent run," it does not require real OS/network/subprocess effects, so System is unproven; it also does not require judging real model output quality/non-determinism, so Agentic is unproven; without proven Agentic boundary, Workflow cannot be crossed. Rejection-gate: disqualifying evidence for higher levels is the behavior’s focus on propagation of previously produced grounding, which can be deterministically asserted with controlled stage outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute deep-dive, evaluate/write-plan, then implement in one orchestrated in-process run using deterministic stage fixtures; assert final `plan` content includes/uses the prior `grounding` artifact from earlier step.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Chosen level remains Integration because the test validates deterministic artifact propagation, not real model quality.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure boundary is implement flow interacting with artifact store under missing-input conditions (fallback path + non-corruption of other artifacts). Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior, so not System; fallback validity here is control-flow/data integrity, not model-output quality, so not Agentic; no multi-agentic sequence needed, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: behavior text targets absence handling and artifact integrity (`grounding is absent`, `without corrupting other stored artifacts`), which is testable with in-process integrated components.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement stage with no `grounding` artifact present and with other artifacts pre-seeded; assert it still creates a syntactically/structurally valid `plan` artifact and leaves unrelated artifacts unchanged.
